GuestProfile.title: GuestProfile
GuestProfile.description: Represents a guest's profile, including personal information, preferences, and linked health data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GuestProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the guest.

lastName.description: The last name of the guest.

email.description: The email address of the guest.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the guest.

dietaryRestrictionIds.description: References to DietaryRestriction entities that the guest adheres to. (Relationship: GuestProfile N:N DietaryRestriction)

allergyIds.description: References to Allergy entities that the guest has. (Relationship: GuestProfile N:N Allergy)

healthDataId.description: Reference to the associated HealthData entity, summarizing the guest's health profile. (Relationship: GuestProfile 1:1 HealthData)

preferredCuisine.description: The guest's preferred type of cuisine.

notes.description: Internal notes from staff about the guest's preferences or behavior.

isVIP.description: Indicates if the guest is considered a Very Important Person (VIP).

loyaltyPoints.description: The current loyalty points accumulated by the guest.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the guest profile was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the guest profile was last updated.

StaffUser.title: StaffUser
StaffUser.description: Represents an internal staff member with role-based access to the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StaffUser entity (corresponds to Firebase Auth UID).

firstName.description: The first name of the staff member.

lastName.description: The last name of the staff member.

email.description: The email address of the staff member.

role.description: The role of the staff member (e.g., 'Admin', 'Chef', 'KitchenManager', 'FrontOfHouse').

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the staff member.

hireDate.description: The date when the staff member was hired.

lastLogin.description: The date and time of the staff member's last login.

isActive.description: Indicates if the staff member's account is currently active.

preferredLanguage.description: The preferred language for the staff member's interface.

Ingredient.title: Ingredient
Ingredient.description: Represents a single culinary ingredient used in the kitchen.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ingredient entity.

name.description: The common name of the ingredient.

description.description: A brief description of the ingredient.

unitOfMeasure.description: The standard unit of measure for this ingredient (e.g., 'gram', 'milliliter', 'each').

supplier.description: The primary supplier for this ingredient.

storageConditions.description: Recommended storage conditions for the ingredient (e.g., 'refrigerated', 'dry storage').

allergyIds.description: References to Allergy entities that this ingredient contains. (Relationship: Ingredient N:N Allergy)

compatibleDietaryRestrictions.description: A list of dietary restrictions this ingredient is compatible with (e.g., 'Vegetarian', 'Vegan').

costPerUnit.description: The cost of one unit of the ingredient.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the ingredient.

isLocalSourced.description: Indicates if the ingredient is sourced locally.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a dish or beverage available on the restaurant's menu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item.

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item.

price.description: The price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the prepared menu item.

preparationTimeMinutes.description: The estimated time in minutes required to prepare the menu item.

ingredientIds.description: References to Ingredient entities used in this menu item. (Relationship: MenuItem N:N Ingredient)

dietaryRestrictionIds.description: References to DietaryRestriction entities that this menu item is compatible with. (Relationship: MenuItem N:N DietaryRestriction)

allergyIds.description: References to Allergy entities that this menu item contains. (Relationship: MenuItem N:N Allergy)

category.description: The category of the menu item (e.g., 'Appetizer', 'Main Course', 'Dessert').

chefRecommendation.description: Indicates if this item is a chef's special recommendation.

isActive.description: Indicates if the menu item is currently available.

nutritionInfoId.description: Reference to the associated NutritionInfo entity for this menu item. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:1 NutritionInfo)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the menu item was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the menu item was last updated.

NutritionInfo.title: NutritionInfo
NutritionInfo.description: Provides detailed nutritional information for a specific menu item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NutritionInfo entity.

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem entity this nutrition information belongs to. (Relationship: NutritionInfo 1:1 MenuItem)

proteinGrams.description: Protein content in grams per serving.

fatGrams.description: Fat content in grams per serving.

carbsGrams.description: Carbohydrate content in grams per serving.

servingSize.description: Description of the serving size (e.g., '1 cup', '1 piece').

updatedAt.description: The date and time when this nutrition information was last updated.

Inventory.title: Inventory
Inventory.description: Tracks the current stock levels and details for a specific ingredient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inventory record.

ingredientId.description: Reference to the Ingredient entity this inventory record is for. (Relationship: Ingredient 1:N Inventory)

quantity.description: The current quantity of the ingredient in stock.

unitOfMeasure.description: The unit of measure for the current quantity (should match ingredient's unit).

minimumStockLevel.description: The minimum desired quantity of the ingredient before reordering.

reorderPoint.description: The stock level at which a reorder alert should be triggered.

lastRestockDate.description: The date and time when the ingredient was last restocked.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the current stock batch.

location.description: The physical storage location of the ingredient (e.g., 'Pantry', 'Fridge A').

status.description: The current stock status (e.g., 'In Stock', 'Low Stock', 'Out of Stock').

updatedAt.description: The date and time when this inventory record was last updated.

WearableData.title: WearableData
WearableData.description: Stores raw, time-series health data collected from a guest's wearable device.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WearableData record.

guestProfileId.description: Reference to the GuestProfile entity this data belongs to. (Relationship: GuestProfile 1:N WearableData)

deviceId.description: Identifier for the wearable device (e.g., 'Galaxy Watch 5', 'Apple Watch Series 8').

dataType.description: The type of health metric recorded (e.g., 'HeartRate', 'GlucoseLevel', 'BloodOxygen').

value.description: The numerical value of the recorded data point.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the value (e.g., 'bpm', 'mg/dL', '%').

timestamp.description: The date and time when the data point was recorded.

HealthData.title: HealthData
HealthData.description: A summarized and consolidated health profile for a guest, potentially derived from wearable data and manual input.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthData entity.

guestProfileId.description: Reference to the associated GuestProfile entity. (Relationship: GuestProfile 1:1 HealthData)

latestGlucoseLevel.description: The guest's most recent glucose level reading.

preExistingConditions.description: A list of any pre-existing medical conditions the guest has.

guestNotes.description: Specific health-related notes provided by the guest or staff.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when this health data was last updated.

DietaryRestriction.title: DietaryRestriction
DietaryRestriction.description: Defines a specific dietary restriction or preference.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DietaryRestriction entity.

name.description: The name of the dietary restriction (e.g., 'Vegetarian', 'Vegan', 'Gluten-Free').

description.description: A description of the dietary restriction.

isCommon.description: Indicates if this is a commonly requested dietary restriction.

Allergy.title: Allergy
Allergy.description: Defines a specific food allergy that guests or ingredients may have.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Allergy entity.

description.description: A description of the allergy and its typical reactions.

severity.description: The typical severity of the allergic reaction (e.g., 'Mild', 'Moderate', 'Severe').

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a guest's food or beverage order placed at the restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

guestProfileId.description: Reference to the GuestProfile entity if the order is associated with a specific guest. (Relationship: GuestProfile 1:N Order)

staffUserId.description: Reference to the StaffUser entity who managed or processed this order. (Relationship: StaffUser 1:N Order)

orderDateTime.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Preparing', 'Ready', 'Served', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

specialInstructions.description: Any special instructions or modifications for the order.

tableNumber.description: The table number where the order is to be served.

menuItemIds.description: References to MenuItem entities included in this order. (Relationship: Order N:N MenuItem)

Reservation.title: Reservation
Reservation.description: Represents a guest's reservation at the restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reservation entity.

guestProfileId.description: Reference to the GuestProfile entity associated with this reservation. (Relationship: GuestProfile 1:N Reservation)

reservationDateTime.description: The date and time for which the reservation is made.

numberOfGuests.description: The number of guests for this reservation.

status.description: The current status of the reservation (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'Seated', 'Cancelled', 'No-Show').

specialRequests.description: Any special requests made for the reservation (e.g., 'window seat', 'high chair').

staffUserId.description: Reference to the StaffUser entity who handled this reservation. (Relationship: StaffUser 1:N Reservation)

tablePreference.description: Any specific table preferences for the reservation.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the reservation was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the reservation was last updated.

definition.description: Stores staff member profiles and roles for role-based access control. `staffUserId` corresponds to Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: staffUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the staff user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores guest profiles. `guestId` corresponds to Firebase Auth UID. Includes denormalized 'id' as `guestId` to simplify authorization rules based on `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: guestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the guest, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores a guest's summarized health profile. This is a 1:1 relationship with GuestProfile. The document ID 'current' is used for a single, predictable health data record per guest. Includes denormalized `guestProfileId` matching the parent `guestId` for authorization independence.

params.name: guestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the guest, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores time-series health data collected from a guest's wearable device. Each document includes denormalized `guestProfileId` matching the parent `guestId` for authorization independence.

params.name: guestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the guest, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: wearableDataId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific wearable data record.

definition.description: Stores guest food and beverage orders. Includes `guestProfileId` and `staffUserId` fields, which are crucial for authorization independence, allowing direct checks in security rules for both guest and staff access without `get()` operations.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Stores guest reservations. Includes `guestProfileId` and `staffUserId` fields, which are crucial for authorization independence, allowing direct checks in security rules for both guest and staff access without `get()` operations.

params.name: reservationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the reservation.

definition.description: Master data for dietary restrictions. Publicly readable for menu filtering and guest preferences; staff-manageable.

params.name: dietaryRestrictionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific dietary restriction.

definition.description: Master data for food allergies. Publicly readable for menu filtering and guest health data; staff-manageable.

params.name: allergyId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific allergy.

definition.description: Master data for culinary ingredients. Publicly readable for menu transparency; staff-manageable (Chef, Kitchen Manager, Admin).

params.name: ingredientId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific ingredient.

definition.description: Master data for menu items. Publicly readable for guests and staff; staff-manageable (Chef, Admin).

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific menu item.

definition.description: Detailed nutritional information for a specific menu item. This is a 1:1 relationship with MenuItem. The document ID 'current' is used for a single, predictable nutrition record per menu item. Includes denormalized `menuItemId` matching the parent `menuItemId` for authorization independence. Publicly readable; staff-manageable.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent menu item.

definition.description: Tracks stock levels for ingredients. Accessible only by authorized staff (Kitchen Manager, Admin) for read and write operations. Includes `ingredientId` for direct reference to the associated ingredient.

params.name: inventoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for an inventory record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Gastronomic AI prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence and Denormalization:**

1.  **Guest-owned Data:** For entities like `GuestProfile`, `HealthData`, and `WearableData`, a hierarchical path structure `/guests/{guestId}/healthData/{healthDataId}` or `/guests/{guestId}/wearableData/{wearableDataId}` is used. Critically, each subcollection document (`healthData`, `wearableData`) will explicitly contain a `guestProfileId` field that mirrors the parent `guestId`. This denormalization ensures that authorization rules can perform direct field checks (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.guestProfileId`) without needing `get()` operations on parent documents, thus preserving atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging.

2.  **Shared/Collaborative Data:** For entities like `Order` and `Reservation`, which involve both a `GuestProfile` and a `StaffUser`, these are placed in root-level collections (`/orders/{orderId}`, `/reservations/{reservationId}`). Each `Order` and `Reservation` document explicitly includes both `guestProfileId` and `staffUserId` fields. This denormalization allows security rules to directly verify access for either the guest owner or the managing staff member, eliminating hierarchical authorization dependencies and enabling secure `list` operations.

3.  **Master Data:** `MenuItem` and `NutritionInfo` are treated similarly. `NutritionInfo` is nested under `MenuItem` (`/menuItems/{menuItemId}/nutritionInfo/{nutritionInfoId}`), and each `NutritionInfo` document will contain its parent `menuItemId` field. This allows both entities to be read by all (guests and staff) but written only by authorized staff, with independent security checks if needed.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**

1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For guest-specific data, the path itself (`/guests/{guestId}/...`) acts as a primary authorization primitive. A guest can only query their own path. For example, a guest requesting their `WearableData` can only do so if `guestId == request.auth.uid` is enforced in the rules. This ensures any `list` operation on these subcollections is implicitly scoped to the authenticated user.

2.  **Denormalized Fields for Root Collections:** For `Order` and `Reservation` collections, which are root-level but have guest-specific access, the denormalized `guestProfileId` field is used. Guests can securely `list` their own orders by adding a `where('guestProfileId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause to their query. Staff can query without this restriction, and security rules will enforce their role-based access for `list` operations.

3.  **Structural Segregation for Public/Internal Data:** Collections like `MenuItem`, `Ingredient`, `DietaryRestriction`, and `Allergy` are designated as 'publicly readable' master data, allowing all users (guests and staff) to perform `list` operations to populate menus or provide dietary information. In contrast, `Inventory` and `StaffUser` data are strictly internal, readable and writable only by authorized staff roles, ensuring that `list` operations on these collections are inherently secure for internal operations. This segregation prevents 'rules as filters' issues by ensuring homogeneous security postures within each collection.

**DBAC (Database-Based Access Control):**

*   The `StaffUser` collection at `/staffUsers/{staffUserId}` centrally stores staff member profiles, with the `staffUserId` mapping directly to the Firebase Auth UID. The `role` field within each `StaffUser` document is the single source of truth for role-based access control. Security rules will use `exists()` and `get()` calls on this collection to verify staff roles before granting elevated permissions, fully avoiding custom claims.

This structure provides clear separation of concerns, explicit authorization context, and robust support for secure `list` operations, making the Firestore rules simpler, more efficient, and easier to debug.
